question: What are current approaches to evaluating tool use in LLM agents?

an aggregator, not a primary source."]},"answer":"## Answer
Current tool-use evaluation for LLM agents is now mostly **multi-layered**: end-to-end task success, structured tool-call correctness, argument validity, recovery under failures, and operational efficiency are evaluated together rather than with a single pass/fail score. The survey literature frames this shift as a move toward more realistic and continuously updated benchmarks, while also noting that cost-efficiency and robustness are still under-measured in many setups. https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.16416

A practical reading of the field is:
- **Task success**: check whether the agent actually completes the user’s objective in the environment, often by comparing final state to a goal state and repeating runs to estimate reliability. https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12045
- **Tool-call correctness / argument validity**: score whether the right tool is chosen and whether the emitted call matches the schema and arguments closely enough to execute. https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-industry.85.pdf https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/evaluation
- **Recovery from tool errors**: stress-test agents by injecting tool/API faults and measuring whether they diagnose the failure and recover. https://openreview.net/pdf?id=3Snm5cHdRu
- **Cost and latency**: log trace-level token usage, cost, and latency alongside quality so teams can trade off performance against runtime economics. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/generative-ai/agent-evaluation/

Engineering inference: the most useful current evaluation stack is a **hybrid** one—state-based success metrics for outcome quality, structured call checks for correctness, fault injection for robustness, and trace-based operational metrics for cost/latency. That combination is better aligned with real deployments than any single benchmark alone. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/evaluation https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/generative-ai/agent-evaluation/

### Required aspect coverage
- task success — τ-bench compares final database state to the goal state and adds pass^k reliability over repeated trials: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12045
- tool-call correctness — BFCL and Microsoft both evaluate whether the right function/tool call is made: https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-industry.85.pdf https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/evaluation
- argument validity — Microsoft’s `tool_call_args_match` and BFCL’s function-name/argument matching directly test argument correctness: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/evaluation https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-industry.85.pdf
- recovery from tool errors — robustness benchmarks inject faults and score recovery rate and error identification: https://openreview.net/pdf?id=3Snm5cHdRu
- cost and latency — Databricks Agent Evaluation exposes quality, cost, and latency from traces: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/generative-ai/agent-evaluation/

## Sources
- Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.16416
- τ-bench: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12045
- BFCL / Berkeley Function Calling Benchmark paper: https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-industry.85.pdf
- Microsoft Agent Framework evaluation docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/evaluation
- Databricks Agent Evaluation docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/generative-ai/agent-evaluation/
- Robustness benchmark with fault injection and recovery metrics: https://openreview.net/pdf?id=3Snm5cHdRu

## Limitations
- The exact metric names and thresholds vary by benchmark and provider, so results are not directly comparable across all frameworks. https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12045 https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-industry.85.pdf
- The recovery source is a recent benchmark paper; it supports the approach, but it is not yet as established as BFCL or τ-bench. https://openreview.net/pdf?id=3Snm5cHdRu
- I did not include broad vendor marketing pages or benchmark aggregators; they were rejected because they were not primary evidence. https://agentv.dev/ https://benchlm.ai/llm-agent-benchmarks"} }
